title: Neoporteria centeteria
taxonomy: Neoporteria centeteria belongs to the family Cactaceae and is a genus of cacti. It is native to the Andes mountains in South America, specifically in Chile and Argentina. This species is part of the subgenus Eriosyce, which is a group of slow-growing cacti. The classification of Neoporteria centeteria is further divided into several sub-species, each with distinct characteristics. Neoporteria centeteria is often found in high-altitude regions, where it thrives in dry and rocky terrain.

intro: Neoporteria centreria, also known as the Chilean torch flower, is a flowering plant native to the Atacama Desert in Chile. Its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neoporteria centreria include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
